candidates.title: Gordie Howe bridge cost still $6.4B despite delays, federal authority says
candidates.description: The cost of the new Gordie Howe bridge connecting Windsor and Detroit is still $6.4 billion despite multiple delays, according to the federal authority that oversees the bridge.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Calgary Stampede parade kicks off 10 days of western fun
candidates.description: A pair of Winter Olympians traded their skis and skates for hats and boots Friday as the Calgary Stampede parade kicked off 10 days of western-themed festivities.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: StubHub sold ‘ghost tickets’ for World Cup months before real ones were issued, CBC finds
candidates.description: StubHub claims it doesn’t allow the listing of “speculative” tickets on its platform. But CBC News found “ghost tickets” were available for the FIFA World Cup months before they were issued — and was able to list non-existent tickets for the 2028 Olympics.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Elections Alberta rejects Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition
candidates.description: Elections Alberta has rejected musician Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition, saying it failed to meet the requirements for a citizen initiative petition.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: More than 2,000 Quebec households stranded without a lease after July 1
candidates.description: More than 2,000 Quebec households are still without a home following the province's July 1 moving day. Housing advocates say the increase compared to last year shows that building more apartments isn't enough to solve the affordability crisis.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: New Brunswick, Prince Edward Island and B.C. assist Quebec in battling wildfires
candidates.description: Three provinces are lending a helping hand to Quebec's wildfire prevention and suppression agency in its efforts to combat fires.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'I will kill everyone around you': Threat to ex by father in double murder-suicide emerges
candidates.description: Police have identified the man who is believed to have killed his seven- and 12-year-old sons in his south Ottawa home and firebombed his dental practice in a nearby village before dying by suicide in a vehicle fire on Monday, as details of his threat to cause bodily harm to his ex-wife come to light.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Greater Toronto home sales increased in June, prices expected to rise in months ahead: real estate board
candidates.description: The Greater Toronto Area continued to see higher home sales last month compared with a year ago even as new listings slowed.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.L.'s Indigenous relations minister says province reviewing Innu timeline
candidates.description: Newfoundland and Labrador's Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Minister Lela Evans says the provincial government is dedicated to "true reconciliation" with Innu people, but stopped short of any endorsement of Innu history in the region extending beyond 300 years.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fatal crash on Fernie, B.C., mountain bike trail shocks riding community
candidates.description: Elk Valley RCMP say a man died after a mountain biking crash on Trail Dogs in Fernie, B.C. Police say there is no indication of criminality, and the B.C. Coroners Service is investigating.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Nearly 600 wildfire evacuees from Kasabonika Lake First Nation staying in Toronto
candidates.description: Kasabonika Lake First Nation in northwestern Ontario has paused an evacuation of its most vulnerable members, as a cluster of five wildfires surrounding the community have stabilized for the time being. Kasabonika Lake’s Chief Matthias Anderson says as of Thursday afternoon, 548 evacuees remain in Toronto and 37 more are on the way from Thunder Bay, making up nearly half the community's population. Here's the latest on the situation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Western Manitoba First Nation declares state of emergency as flooding sparks calls for federal aid
candidates.description: Minegoziibe Anishinabe First Nation, in Manitoba’s flood-stricken Parkland area, has declared a state of emergency due to washed out infrastructure, joining more than 30 communities that have done the same.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fed up with apartment problems, these tenants made a zine
candidates.description: Led by artist Sonali Menezes, the tenant association of a Hamilton apartment complex created Welcome to Lamoreaux Gardens. The publication includes images of pest infestations, leaking pipes and overflowing garbage.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How can Canada beat Morocco? It could be found in Promise David’s sublime group-stage goal
candidates.description: If there’s a reason for Canada’s men to believe they can beat Morocco on Saturday, it’s most easily found in Promise David’s singular moment against Switzerland.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario pushed ahead with strong mayor powers expansion despite 'predominantly negative' feedback
candidates.description: Premier Doug Ford’s sweeping expansion of strong mayor powers to nearly half of Ontario’s municipalities was met with "predominantly negative" feedback during consultations, with respondents calling the system “unnecessary” and “undemocratic," documents obtained by CBC News reveal.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Want to cool off with a dip in a river or lake? Here's how to stay safe
candidates.description: After a rash of drowning deaths during Europe's ongoing record-breaking heat wave, advocates are urging Canadians to stay safe this Canada Day as they push for more public swimming access across the country.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Growing recognition for Inuk war hero John Shiwak brings ‘sense of relief’ says relative
candidates.description: Even though he died more than a century ago, fighting far from home, seeing Inuk war hero John Shiwak's growing recognition for his sacrifice is a relief for his family.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gaza has become frozen in 'humanitarian purgatory' — and it could get worse
candidates.description: Nine months after Hamas and Israel agreed to a hard-fought ceasefire in Gaza, the term has become a cruel irony for the two million Palestinians trapped inside the territory.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'Our families were so scared': Indian sea captain fears for other seafarers still stuck in Persian Gulf
candidates.description: An Indian sea captain who was stuck on an oil tanker in the Persian Gulf for more than two months as the U.S. and Israel-Iran war raged says thousands of mariners are still at risk and don’t get the recognition they deserve.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran begins week of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
candidates.description: The body of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei lay in state in a vast hall in Tehran on Friday as clerics, officials, foreign dignitaries and other mourners paid their respects to Iran's late Supreme Leader, killed in a U.S.-Israeli airstrike earlier this year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Interpol names suspect in Monaco bombing that reportedly wounded Russia-linked tycoon
candidates.description: Interpol on Friday identified a 39-year-old woman from Ukraine as the main suspect in a bombing in Monaco that is thought to have targeted a Ukrainian tycoon with links to Russia.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump isn't extending CUSMA trade deal, so what happens now?
candidates.description: The Trump administration surprised no one with its long-expected announcement Wednesday that the U.S. would not join Canada and Mexico in extending the free trade deal between the three countries. Where things go from here, however, is anyone's guess.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ex-Olympian indicted on felony charge over what Trump called reflecting pool vandalism
candidates.description: A former U.S. Olympian was indicted Thursday on a felony charge in what President Donald Trump has called vandalism of the reflecting pool.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Deadly, large-scale Russian attack shakes Kyiv as Ukrainian strikes batter Moscow's oil sector
candidates.description: Russia hammered Kyiv with an 11-hour drone and missile attack overnight into Thursday morning, killing at least 21 civilians in the city and injuring scores more, in what Moscow said was retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil facilities.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Watchdog puts blame on U.S. Secret Service for missing crucial radio calls during 2024 Trump shooting
candidates.description: The U.S. Secret Service did not receive more than 100 local radio transmissions about the gunman who attempted to assassinate President Donald Trump at a 2024 campaign rally in Butler, Pa., ‌according to a government watchdog report released on Thursday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran warns oil tankers to use approved routes in Strait of Hormuz or face 'forceful response'
candidates.description: Iran's joint military command warned Thursday that all oil tankers moving through the Strait of Hormuz must use its approved routes or face a "forceful response," again ratcheting up tensions over a waterway crucial for international energy supplies.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 9 monks struck and killed in Thailand by 11-year-old driver
candidates.description: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck into a group of monks on a pilgrimage walk in northeastern Thailand on Thursday, killing nine, officials said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Soccer fans file class-action lawsuit against StubHub over cancelled World Cup tickets
candidates.description: Soccer fans in the U.S. are suing StubHub, accusing the resale platform of failing to deliver expensive tickets for the FIFA World Cup that they bought on the secondary market.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why one superfan thinks now is the right time for Canada's Eurovision debut
candidates.description: When it was announced that Canada would be entering the 2027 Eurovision Song Contest — it was music to Matti McLean’s ears. A Canadian superfan of the international music competition, co-host of the Think About Eurovision podcast and creator of the Fringe Festival show A Canadian Explains Eurovision to Other Canadians, McLean says he has been campaigning for years to see this moment become a reality.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada Post stopping parcel delivery to some EU countries because of new duty
candidates.description: Canada Post says it is suspending service to some European Union countries over new customs rules for low-value shipments, saying it's not accepting parcels destined for a dozen countries until further notice.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta pitches southern route for West Coast pipeline, with a price tag of $35B or more
candidates.description: Alberta is formally proposing an oil pipeline to the southwest coast of British Columbia for tanker export to Asian markets, in a partnership with federally-owned Trans Mountain Corp. and Calgary-based Pembina Pipeline Corp.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hoekstra says no 'significant progress' in 14 months of trade negotiations, but U.S. to keep talking
candidates.description: The U.S. envoy to Canada says 14 months of trade talk between Canada and the Trump administration has resolved few issues. But Pete Hoekstra insists the Americans will keep talking in the hopes of striking an agreement on the future of CUSMA, as well as settling other issues.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Green light for Greenlight: Pembina, partners go ahead with gas plant for data centre
candidates.description: Pembina Pipeline Corp. and two partners have given the go-ahead to the Greenlight Electricity Centre, a natural gas plant serving a data centre customer. The 932-megawatt plant would be built in Sturgeon County, part of Alberta's Industrial Heartland region north of Edmonton.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Sony to end releasing PlayStation games on disc, earning ire from gamers despite market shift to digital
candidates.description: Sony will stop producing discs for physical copies of all new PlayStation video games starting in 2028, the company announced Wednesday, citing customers' growing preference to buy and download their games online.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Shipping costs soar as retailers try to secure orders ahead of fresh tariffs
candidates.description: Retailers are rushing to import products ahead of possible new tariffs expected to land at the end of July. That's pushing the cost to move products by boat way up — and industry experts say consumers will bear the brunt of that extra cost.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
candidates.description: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: German row over plan for workers to need sick note on first day of illness
candidates.description: A doctors' group says it "borders on madness" that patients will have to obtain the note in person.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
candidates.description: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
candidates.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Stand-up comic held for jokes about Erdoğan and Islam in Turkey crackdown
candidates.description: Deniz Göktaş is regarded as one of Turkey's most popular comedians and is accused of "inciting hatred and hostility" on stage.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Dissident Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies aged 70
candidates.description: Lam Wing-kee, who defied China by selling material critical of Beijing, passed away after suffering from lung cancer.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: A mayor in Japan announced her maternity leave - and got the whole country talking
candidates.description: In the face of criticism, Shoko Kawata, 35, says she loves her job and is proud to be taking time off to have a baby.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 30
candidates.description: The latest barrage deployed the largest number of weapons on the capital and hit locations over a wide area.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
candidates.description: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Ebola treatments trial begins in DR Congo
candidates.description: According to WHO data, there have been 1,406 confirmed cases of the disease in DRC, with 301 suspected cases and 438 deaths.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Killers of British couple in South Africa sentenced to life
candidates.description: The pair had been collecting rare plants and seeds before their bodies were found days later in a river.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
candidates.description: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool
candidates.description: US authorities charged him with property destruction after renovations championed by President Donald Trump appeared to go awry.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
candidates.description: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: On the Strait of Hormuz, BBC finds seized ships and shark fishermen as uneasy calm returns
candidates.description: The BBC travels to the city of Bandar Abbas - the first time journalists from a UK broadcaster have been to the Iranian side of the strait.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
candidates.description: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: After 250 years the American Dream is surviving, but only just
candidates.description: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
candidates.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What will guests wear to Taylor Swift's wedding?
candidates.description: Romantic, fairytale vibes could dominate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported wedding event in New York City on Friday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Turkish police beat us with iron rods before we lost limbs to frostbite, Afghans say
candidates.description: The young migrants heading to Europe say they were stripped and had their hands tied in freezing temperatures.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation
candidates.description: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Banned by Beijing, this comedian is taking his act to Chinese speakers abroad
candidates.description: Chizi, one of China’s biggest stand-up comedians, has found a new stage after falling afoul of China's censors.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canada celebrates its birthday as Mark Carney battles to keep it intact
candidates.description: As Canadians celebrate their nation's birthday, independence movements in two provinces are eyeing the exit from the country.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
candidates.description: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
candidates.description: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Beautiful and terrible' - the world's thoughts on the US at 250
candidates.description: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Parents warned not to publicly share children’s images amid AI abuse risks
candidates.description: The NCA says there is a growing threat of children's images being used to create child abuse material.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Meta glasses wearers hit with paywall to use built-in feature
candidates.description: A feature in Meta's glasses which boosts the voice of people will be capped at three hours for free use.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Google must pay €4.1bn fine for using Android to 'block' rivals
candidates.description: A Google spokesperson said the judgement "fails to recognise" the firm's "significant investment to ensure Android remains open."
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
candidates.description: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: July 4th events threatened by heat wave. And, Russia strikes on Ukraine's capital
candidates.description: July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a trouble-making seal
candidates.description: The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Ex-HK bookseller Lam Wing-kee, detained by China in 2015, dies in Taiwan at 70
candidates.description: Lam was one of five people from Causeway Bay Books who disappeared in 2015. He later detailed his detention by Chinese authorities.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Are the wars in Ukraine and Iran showing the limits of global powers?
candidates.description: We look at the Russian attacks on Kyiv and the overall state of the wars between Russia and Ukraine and the U.S. and Iran.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
candidates.description: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
candidates.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Vatican declares Society of St. Pius X in schism, excommunicates bishops
candidates.description: The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 'We can still pull out live victims': LA rescuers join Venezuela quake rescue efforts
candidates.description: In Venezuela, international emergency crews — including a team from LA — are working tirelessly to reach people still missing after last week's devastating earthquakes.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
candidates.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
candidates.description: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump earned more than $1 billion from crypto — but his investors didn't fare so well
candidates.description: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures. But there are thousands of investors who bought into some of those cryptocurrencies, and they stand to lose big.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. job market slows in June
candidates.description: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
candidates.description: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
candidates.description: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
candidates.description: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe
candidates.description: Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse
candidates.description: Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
candidates.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: As a heat wave grips the U.S., a look at how extreme heat affects the body
candidates.description: As heat waves sweep the country, NPR's A Martinez asks W. Larry Kenney, professor of physiology and kinesiology at Penn State University, how extreme heat affects humans.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 3 at 4:36PM EDT by NWS Peachtree City GA
candidates.description: At 436 PM EDT, a strong thunderstorm was near Holland, or near Summerville, moving west at 5 mph. HAZARD...40 to 50 mph wind, up to penny sized hail and frequent cloud to ground lightning. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect minor damage to tree limbs and blowing around of light, unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Summerville, Lyerly, James H. Floyd State Park, Berryton, and Holland.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 4:36PM EDT until July 3 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Peachtree City GA
candidates.description: SVRFFC The National Weather Service in Peachtree City has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southeastern Floyd County in northwestern Georgia... Northeastern Polk County in northwestern Georgia... Southwestern Bartow County in northwestern Georgia... * Until 500 PM EDT. * At 436 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Euharlee, or 9 miles west of Cartersville, moving southwest at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Cartersville, Rockmart, Euharlee, Aragon, Kingston, Taylorsville, Stilesboro, and Lindale.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch issued July 3 at 3:35PM CDT until July 3 at 11:00PM CDT by NWS Hastings NE
candidates.description: THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS ISSUED SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WATCH 449 IN EFFECT UNTIL 11 PM CDT THIS EVENING FOR THE FOLLOWING AREAS IN NEBRASKA THIS WATCH INCLUDES 7 COUNTIES IN CENTRAL NEBRASKA MERRICK NANCE IN EAST CENTRAL NEBRASKA POLK YORK IN SOUTH CENTRAL NEBRASKA FILLMORE HAMILTON THAYER THIS INCLUDES THE CITIES OF AURORA, CENTRAL CITY, DESHLER, EXETER, FAIRMONT, FULLERTON, GENEVA, GENOA, HEBRON, OSCEOLA, POLK, SHELBY, STROMSBURG, AND YORK.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 3:35PM CDT until July 3 at 4:00PM CDT by NWS Bismarck ND
candidates.description: At 335 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 12 miles south of Beulah, moving northeast at 10 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Mainly rural areas of Southwestern Mercer and Western Oliver Counties.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 4:34PM EDT until July 3 at 6:00PM EDT by NWS Grand Rapids MI
candidates.description: SVRGRR The National Weather Service in Grand Rapids has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Jackson County in south central Michigan... * Until 600 PM EDT. * At 434 PM EDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 13 miles south of Eaton Rapids to near Albion, moving northeast at 15 mph. HAZARD...70 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect considerable tree damage. Wind damage is also likely to mobile homes, roofs, and outbuildings. * Locations impacted include... Jackson... Brooklyn... Grass Lake... Vandercook Lake... Leoni... Spring Arbor... Napoleon... Sandstone... Norvell... Pulaski... Concord... Horton... Parma... Hanover... Jackson County Airpo... Oak Point... Pleasant Lake... Liberty... Vineyard Lake... Clark Lake...
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 2:34PM MDT until July 3 at 3:30PM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
candidates.description: SVRTFX The National Weather Service in Great Falls has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southeastern Fergus County in central Montana... * Until 330 PM MDT. * At 233 PM MDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 11 miles southwest of Grass Range, or 26 miles southeast of Lewistown, moving east at 30 mph. HAZARD...Ping pong ball size hail and 60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...People and animals outdoors will be injured. Expect hail damage to roofs, siding, windows, and vehicles. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Grass Range.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 2:34PM MDT until July 3 at 3:00PM MDT by NWS North Platte NE
candidates.description: At 233 PM MDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Hay Springs, moving southeast at 15 mph. HAZARD...Ping pong ball size hail and 60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...People and animals outdoors will be injured. Expect hail damage to roofs, siding, windows, and vehicles. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Rushville, Hay Springs, Metcalf State Wildlife Management Area, Intersection of Beaver Creek Road and White Clay Creek Road, Intersection of Highway 87 and Larabee Road, Intersection of Highway 87 and Antelope Road, Walgren Lake State Recreation Area, and Mount Maria. This includes the following highways... Highway 20 between mile markers 74 and 95. Highway 250 between mile markers 45 and 48.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 4:33PM EDT until July 3 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Binghamton NY
candidates.description: At 433 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Binghamton, moving east at 25 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Binghamton, Johnson City, Kirkwood, Conklin, Susquehanna, Deposit, Susquehanna Depot, Port Dickinson, Hallstead, and Windsor.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 3 at 3:33PM CDT until July 4 at 8:00PM CDT by NWS Wichita KS
candidates.description: * WHAT...Heat index values up to 105 expected. * WHERE...Allen, Chautauqua, Elk, Greenwood, Labette, Montgomery, Neosho, Wilson, and Woodson Counties. * WHEN...From noon to 8 PM CDT Saturday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 3 at 3:33PM CDT until July 3 at 3:45PM CDT by NWS Nashville TN
candidates.description: At 332 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 10 miles north of Pulaski, and is nearly stationary. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Pulaski, Cornersville, Lynnville, Goodspring, and Culleoka. This includes Interstate 65 between mile markers 16 and 27.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: M 3.8 - 2 km E of Oak Harbor, Washington
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 3.8. The event occurred near 2 km E of Oak Harbor, Washington. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
